name: history-workflow
description: Histories sample a single quantity at a common interval during cycling — used for convergence checks, load/displacement curves, joint force/slip response, pore-pressure and dynamic traces. 3DEC samples from blocks, block contacts (joints), the global model, FISH symbols, and structural elements; results are listed/plotted (chart-history) and exported as time/step series.

sampling_targets.samples: Deformable-block zone / gridpoint field at the nearest location — stress, displacement, velocity, strain-rate, pore-pressure, temperature, etc.
sampling_targets.field_names: Same vocabulary as plot-items: see references/plot-items 'block contour' / 'bzone contour'.

sampling_targets.samples: Joint (sub-contact) response — force-normal, force-shear, stress-normal, stress-shear, displacement-normal, displacement-shear[-x/-y/-z], area, *-sum aggregates.
sampling_targets.field_names: force-normal, force-shear, force-normal-sum, force-shear-sum, stress-normal, stress-shear[-x/-y/-z], displacement-normal, displacement-shear[-maximum/-x/-y/-z], velocity-shear[-x/-y/-z], area, area-sum.

sampling_targets.samples: Global scalars — mechanical (unbalanced force ratio), energy, timestep, damping, dynamic time, creep, fluid, thermal.
sampling_targets.field_names: creep, damping, dynamic, energy, fluid, mechanical, thermal, timestep.

sampling_targets.samples: The value of a FISH symbol each interval — for custom/derived monitoring quantities.

management_keywords.description: Set the cycle/step interval at which all histories sample.
List defined histories (ids, sampled quantity, location).
Write history series to a table or text file (for validation/plotting).
Discard stored samples while keeping the history definitions.

notes: Sampling sub-forms 'block gridpoint history <field>' and 'structure history <type> ...' (beam/cable/liner/pile/shell/geogrid/node/link) also exist (bridge-probed); SELs are not standalone command-corpus pages.
Field/quantity names for block sampling are the same set documented under plot-items contour — query references/plot-items instead of duplicating them here.
Plot a history with 'plot item create chart-history'.
